seoTitle: How to Create a Zendesk Ticket From Email Automatically
metaDescription: Learn how to route inbound emails into Zendesk tickets automatically, from setting up a support address to verifying forwarding and routing rules.
intro: Manually copying messages out of a shared inbox wastes time and loses conversation history. Zendesk can automatically convert inbound emails into structured support tickets once you connect a support address. This guide covers setting up a native or external address and verifying the connection.

steps.heading: Open the Email channel panel
steps.body: In Admin Center, expand the Channels directory and click the Email panel.

steps.heading: Set up a native or external address
steps.body: Click Add Address to create a native Zendesk subdomain alias, or choose Connect External Address to link an existing company mailbox.

steps.heading: Configure mail forwarding
steps.body: If using an external address, set up your mail server to forward messages to the unique Zendesk token provided.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Run the verification checks inside Zendesk to confirm your routing connection is active.

steps.heading: Set routing triggers
steps.body: Open Objects and Rules to configure action triggers that assign target groups and priority for tickets coming from this inbox.

steps.heading: Test with a real email
steps.body: Send a message to your new support address and confirm it appears as a properly formatted ticket.

faq.question: Can I keep my existing company email address?
faq.answer: Yes, connecting an external address lets you preserve your brand's existing domain while routing mail into Zendesk.

faq.question: What if my forwarded emails aren't creating tickets?
faq.answer: Double-check the forwarding rule on your external mail server and confirm the verification token was successfully validated.

faq.question: Can different support addresses get different routing rules?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pair each address with its own trigger conditions to set specific group owners or priority levels.

faq.question: Does converting email to tickets lose the original message history?
faq.answer: No, the original email thread is preserved within the ticket conversation.
